Titre: 1 star
Commentaire:
1 star — completely unacceptable.
Air Algérie diverted my flight from Heathrow to Stansted without notifying me at all. I only found out through a friend, not the airline. That alone says everything about their communication — or lack of it.

I live near Heathrow, so being forced to travel an extra hour to Stansted was a major disruption. Worse still, I was traveling with my special needs daughter who requires a wheelchair. The airline showed zero consideration for passengers with disabilities or families with additional needs. Getting to Stansted is a serious challenge for us.

No warning, no assistance, no empathy. This experience was stressful, exhausting, and completely avoidable. I would strongly advise disabled passengers and families to avoid Air Algérie.

Titre: Worst airline in Africa
Commentaire:
My checked bag went missing after my flight with Air Algérie, and after some time the airline told me that it had been found at Béjaïa Airport. They said it would be sent to my home address, since I had already left Algeria. After that message, everything stopped.

I have been trying to contact Air Algérie since the summer — emails, forms, messages — and I have not received a single reply. No update about my bag, no explanation, no follow-up. Months have passed and the airline continues to ignore every attempt I make to resolve the situation.

Losing a bag can happen with any airline, but the complete silence after confirming they had located it is unacceptable. My belongings have still not been returned, and I have had to deal with this entirely on my own.

This has been one of the worst customer service experiences I have ever had, and I am sharing this so others are aware of how Air Algérie handles situations when something goes wrong.

Commentaire: Honestly a disaster!! I haven't used Air Algerie for over 15 years. I gave it ago today, and it was a catastrophic experience from start to end. Today the flight was on time, however, 1st class no signs or any indications to board as 1st class, we only been instructed to line up all in one lane and aggressive behaviour from the old lady leading the team, I hesitated to ask, but than had to and as the queue was too long etc... I was told yeah its ok to jump the queue but no organisation or anythjng... But importantly is once again the under performance of the staff not qualified to do the job to my opinion in all areas, it's obvious they are not scared of getting disciplined or reprimanded they make you feel they own the place and us customers we are under valued in fact we are nothing! The plane was very poor standards very old dirty everywhere and the shock is with all honesty from the start soon as we boarded the guy in charge Sat on a chair had coffee and kept talking to a couple of stewards they go n come back than that corner right behind the co pit turned into a meeting room they were 4 of them sat talking even when the pilot announced we are descending where the procedure is to raise the curtains and staff to lead by example after checking passengers and seat belt etc no no no one was checking they put the curtain down so that they dont get disturbed kept talking until nearly the end... It was disrespectful and unprofessional! I will never ever use Air Algerie again simple! Today's flight Algiers to London Heathrow....

Titre: Horrible experience…
Commentaire:
I am writing to express my deep dissatisfaction with the treatment my mother and I received during a recent flight check-in experience with your airline.

My mother, an elderly and disabled woman who requires a wheelchair, was subjected to unnecessary stress and humiliation at the check-in counter. Her passport includes the abbreviation “Vve” to indicate that she is a widow, as is standard in French documentation. Unfortunately, your staff seemed completely unprepared to understand or process this minor detail. They questioned the validity of her passport over those three small letters and nearly denied her boarding as a result.

Despite my calm attempts to explain that “Vve” simply stands for “veuve,” meaning “widow,” the agent showed little understanding or empathy. This situation alone was distressing enough for my mother, who cannot stand for extended periods and was visibly uncomfortable.

To make matters worse, another staff member came over and, in an extremely disrespectful manner, instructed my mother to sit on the luggage scale. Asking an elderly woman to sit on a scale in full view of other passengers is not only inappropriate but also deeply humiliating. This completely disregarded her dignity.

The same staff then insisted on taking a photo of my mother’s passport. I explained that this was sensitive personal information and that such an action was inappropriate. The agent acknowledged my concern but still refused to proceed without taking the photo.

This entire ordeal was distressing, unprofessional, and unacceptable. My mother was left in tears, and I was shocked at how your staff handled the situation.

I must ask: Is this how Air Algeria treats elderly and disabled passengers? Why are staff members not trained to recognize common international document notations, or to handle such matters with care and respect?

Titre: The worst airline ever!
Commentaire:
The worst airline ever!
I travel every now and then to Algeria to visit my family. I usually take Delta from the US to France then take this awful airline to a city called Annaba (I don't have other choice, but this airline).
It is, definitely, the hardest part of my travel.
Dirty: you can't even see through the windows, seats are dirty and broken...
Crew: They don't speak English. Who the hell still doesn't speak English in the world
DELAYS: Always. Last flight was delayed by 5 hours with no apology. They announced it right by the check-in time!!
PEOPLE: Not organized. They can't even form a line and they would come in front of you with no apology.
FYI: I am originally from Algeria, but traveling to that country with Air Algeria makes me wanna throw up. Avoid this airline at any costs. I've never seen an airline worse than this one!
